evidence: The lilac question-mark match card is the single focus; the two periwinkle feature stacks and their arrows serve as supporting context feeding into it.
Elements build progressively across frames — two stacks, then the readout card — never exceeding four novel ideas at equal weight in any single frame.
Each feature name (loud, fast, sad, bright, more) sits directly in its stack under the LISTENER or SONG header; no legend or key panel is present.
All text is labels or key terms (headers, feature names, the '?' readout); no spoken-register prose sits alone on the background.
Headers, feature words, and the question mark are all rendered large and readable, well above the ~2.2% frame-height threshold.
Both stacks, the arrows, and the readout card sit entirely inside the frame with generous margins.
No text or arrow overlaps another element; arrowheads terminate cleanly at the card edges without touching the labels.
Feature names are consistent periwinkle blue and the match readout is lilac, matching the described observed/parameter roles with no color switching.
Every element — headers, feature stacks, arrows, and the '?' card — carries propositional meaning; no decorative filler is present.
The frames deliver the intent: two side-by-side periwinkle feature stacks for listener and song with a lilac match readout holding a question mark and half the frame left empty.
Feature names sit tightly under their headers and arrows connect both stacks directly to the readout card between them.
The sequence builds in reasoning order — headers, then features row by row, then the readout card and arrows — as a continuous construction.
The composition keeps wide margins and a deliberately empty lower-center region, giving a clear reading path from stacks down into the readout.
